channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran block oil exports as rest of the world takes economic hit
item.description: In the U.S.-Iran showdown in the Gulf, the question is: Who can hold out longer? Both countries are now blocking oil exports through the critical Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: In the U.S.-Iran showdown in the Gulf, the question is: Who can hold out longer? Both countries are now blocking oil exports through the critical Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Sen. Chris Van Hollen, D-Md., discusses the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen of Maryland, who serves on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, about the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez asks Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen of Maryland, who serves on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, about the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Why Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales chose to resign
item.description: House Ethics Committee member Rep. Suhas Subramanyam and law professor Richard Painter break down why controversy might have forced Representatives Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales to resign.
item.content:encoded:
House Ethics Committee member Rep. Suhas Subramanyam and law professor Richard Painter break down why controversy might have forced Representatives Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales to resign.

(Image credit: Etienne Laurent/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Reps. Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales are stepping down amid misconduct allegations, the U.S. and Iran are both blocking oil exports, Trump deletes controversial post amid row with pope.
item.content:encoded: Reps. Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales are stepping down amid misconduct allegations, the U.S. and Iran are both blocking oil exports, Trump deletes controversial post amid row with pope.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: SCOTUSblog author Sarah Isgur talks about her new book, 'The Last Branch Standing'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with SCOTUSblog editor and author Sarah Isgur about "The Last Branch Standing," her new book on the Supreme Court.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with SCOTUSblog editor and author Sarah Isgur about "The Last Branch Standing," her new book on the Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Israel and Lebanon to hold first direct talks in decades
item.description: Diplomats from Israel and Lebanon will meet in Washington for rare direct talks.
item.content:encoded: Diplomats from Israel and Lebanon will meet in Washington for rare direct talks.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: 'People had hope': Iranian Americans watch the Iran war from afar
item.description: Americans with ties to Iran open up about the challenges and tensions around the fragile ceasefire.
item.content:encoded: Americans with ties to Iran open up about the challenges and tensions around the fragile ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Kelly McEvers

item.title: Ukraine hopes for more cooperation after Hungary elects new prime minister
item.description: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.
item.content:encoded: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: 'Hanging by a thread': Fertilizer shortage in India deepens due to ongoing Iran war
item.description: With shipments through the Strait of Hormuz stalled after the Iran war began, fertilizer shortages are deepening across India.
item.content:encoded: With shipments through the Strait of Hormuz stalled after the Iran war began, fertilizer shortages are deepening across India.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Remembering former 'Morning Edition' technical director Brian Jarboe
item.description: We remember former Morning Edition technical director Brian Jarboe, who died Monday.
item.content:encoded: We remember former Morning Edition technical director Brian Jarboe, who died Monday.
item.dc:creator: Hosts
